chief_complaint: A 36-year-old Hispanic man presents with chest pain concerning for myocardial infarction.
history: The patient developed a quotidian fever pattern following an upper respiratory tract infection with no significant past medical history.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned in the case report.
labs: Cardiac catheterization revealed non-obstructive coronary artery disease, myopericarditis, significant neutrophilic leukocytosis, and hyperferritinemia.
